channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: 12 Simple Python AI Starter Projects for Beginners
item.description: Starting your AI coding journey does not mean jumping straight into deep learning. Here is a curated list of approachable Python projects, ordered from easiest to slightly more involved. All use...

item.title: Password Spray Attacks: How Attackers Exploit Authentication Weaknesses
item.description: Password spraying is a type of account takeover (ATO) attack in which cybercriminals test one or a small number of commonly used passwords against a large number of user accounts. Password...

item.title: Як я побудував AI-агента для e-commerce: уроки розробки та деплою
item.description: Сьогодні AI-агенти — це вже не фантастика, а необхідність для бізнесу, що працює 24/7. Нещодавно я пройшов шлях від ідеї до реалізації власного AI-агентного рішення для e-commerce (зокрема, для...

item.title: I bundled email + phone validation into one $0 API (because paying for two subscriptions is silly)
item.description: Every signup form needs the same two checks: is this email real, and is this phone number real? Yet the API market makes you buy them separately - one vendor for email verification, another for...

item.title: Why API integrations break when your app scales
item.description: API integrations usually feel easy at the beginning. A few requests, some authentications, maybe a webhook or two and everything works fine. Then your app grows; more users, more traffic, more...

item.title: Extended RUM in DocumentDB: B-tree-like ordered scans for flexible BSON in PostgreSQL
item.description: The main challenge in document databases is the flexible nature of fields: the same path can be a scalar, an array, nested, or missing. Despite this, an index must specify what it covers and the...

item.title: I Built a Free Tool to Verify Aadhaar eSign PDFs — Here's Why It Matters
item.description: A few weeks ago I got a signed contract PDF from someone. It had a little stamp in the corner: "Digitally Signed by Rahul Sharma, Date: 2026-06-01." I had absolutely no idea if that signature was...

item.title: Sync PHP Developer হিসেবে Async PHP বুঝতে গিয়ে যা শিখলাম (Fibers, epoll, আর PHP 8.6)
item.description: আমি গত কয়েক বছর ধরে Laravel-এ কাজ করি। পুরোপুরি sync PHP। Request আসে, process হয়, response যায়। Async জিনিসটা কোনোদিন লাগেনি, তাই শিখিওনি। তারপর একদিন JustSteveKing-এর একটা article পড়লাম PHP...

item.title: The Hidden Security and Performance Mistakes Killing Your Python, PHP, and JavaScript Projects
item.description: Every week, another company makes headlines for a data breach or a site that crawled to a halt under normal traffic. In most cases, the root cause was not some sophisticated attack or freak server...

item.title: May the STAR Be With You: Crafting Winning Behavioural Interview Answers
item.description: The Quest Begins (The "Why") I still remember my first behavioural interview like it was yesterday. I walked in, palms sweaty, ready to talk about my favorite projects, and the interviewer hit me...

item.title: The UK’s generational tobacco ban might not work. I’m supporting it anyway.
item.description: As the parent of two little girls, I often think about how their childhood is different from mine. The seven-year-old is learning about AI at school. The five-year-old is given internet-based...

item.title: PamStealer Uses Fake Maccy Sites and PAM Checks to Steal Mac Login Passwords
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new macOS information stealer called PamStealer that employs a series of clever tricks to infect systems and siphon sensitive data. The stealer, discovered...

item.title: Sony’s PlayStation disc factory is already being repurposed
item.description: The video game disc is dead, and Sony's been planning to kill it for some time, according to a report out of Austria. The man who leads Sony's discmaking operations, Sony DADC president Dietmar...

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg tells staff that AI agents haven’t progressed as quickly as he’d hoped
item.description: At an internal meeting, the Meta CEO reportedly said that AI development efforts were not moving as quickly as anticipated.

item.title: Tesla driver faces manslaughter charges over Texas crash that killed a woman inside her home
item.description: The man whose Tesla struck and killed a woman inside her Texas home last month is now facing manslaughter charges, as reported earlier by The Wall Street Journal and local news outlet KHOU 11....

item.title: Meta has a new app called Pocket that is absolutely nothing like the old Pocket
item.description: Mozilla shut down the well-loved read-it-later Pocket app last year, and now Meta is launching an app called Pocket with an entirely different, AI-focused pitch: this new app lets you make and...

item.title: The best July 4th sales we found so far
item.description: July 4th sales are typically a precursor to what we’d see during a mid-July Prime Day, but obviously things are flipped around this year. Last week’s big Prime Day sale is over, yet there are a...

item.title: Jersey Mike’s IPO illustrates how bad the AI hype has become
item.description: Just for kicks, I took a look at Jersey Mike's IPO documents. Surely a sandwich shop would have no need to mention AI. But lo and behold.

item.title: Newly discovered PamStealer isn't your typical macOS malware
item.description: The discovery underscores the increased effort being poured into Mac infostealers.

item.title: FBI Seizes NetNut Proxy Platform, Popa Botnet
item.description: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) said today it worked with industry partners to seize hundreds of domains associated with NetNut, a sprawling residential proxy service operated by the...

item.title: Google Disrupts NetNut Residential Proxy Network Spanning 2 Million Home Devices
item.description: Google has significantly degraded NetNut, one of the biggest networks that turns home devices into rented relays for other people's traffic. Working with the FBI, Lumen, and others, Google's...

item.title: Meta quietly launches vibe-coded gaming app Pocket
item.description: Meta has quietly launched Pocket, an experimental AI app that lets users generate and share interactive mini games using text prompts.

item.title: Anthropic is discussing a new custom chip with Samsung
item.description: The news comes about a week after OpenAI announced its own custom AI chip in a partnership with Broadcom.

item.title: Ransomware Groups Turn to Citrix Bleed 2, BYOVD, and Supply Chain Credentials
item.description: Threat actors associated with the Anubis ransomware operation have been observed exploiting the Citrix Bleed 2 (CVE-2025-5777) vulnerability to obtain initial access. "Although tactics differ...

item.title: Weber marks down grills and griddles to their best prices ever for July 4th
item.description: If our recent Decoder interview with Weber Blackstone CEO Roger Dahle has you craving freshly grilled meats or veggies, Weber just so happens to have a variety of grills, smokers, griddles, and...

item.title: Can Cursor Remain a Platform for OpenAI and Anthropic’s Models Inside SpaceX?
item.description: Cursor hopes to continue offering third-party AI models after it's acquired by SpaceX, testing the relationships between frontier AI labs.

item.title: The video game disc is dead
item.description: For decades, to be a gamer was to accumulate a lot of stuff. Consoles, controllers, accessories, weird VR gloves that never worked properly, but mostly the games themselves. Over the years, games...

item.title: Influencer screenings aren’t going away
item.description: For a few days, it seemed like Universal decided that there would be no advanced screenings of Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey for influencers. But on Monday, influencers sat alongside traditional...

item.title: Achieving operational excellence with AI
item.description: Frameworks like Lean Six Sigma and business process management (BPM) first gained traction because they promised clarity in the chaos—a structured way to bring order to messy, sprawling...

item.title: ThreatsDay: AI Compute Hijacking, Apple Email Flaw, BlueHammer Ransomware + 14 Stories
item.description: This week’s security news is mostly about weak spots. Browsers, bots, sandboxes, AI systems, and email flows all show the same problem in different ways. Everything looks normal until someone...
